_metadata.disclaimer: Actions are based on existing government guidelines and disaster management SOPs. AI does NOT invent actions — it selects the relevant guideline, localizes it, and simplifies it for the user.
sources: NDMA Guidelines on Heatwaves (ndma.gov.in, Heat Action Plans)
NDMA Guidelines on Cold Waves (ndma.gov.in, IMD)
CPCB National Air Quality Index Guidelines (cpcb.nic.in)
MoHFW Public Health Advisory for Floods (mohfw.gov.in)
MoHFW Public Health Advisory for Heatwaves (mohfw.gov.in)
INCOIS Tsunami Early Warning System Advisories (incois.gov.in)
_metadata.selection_logic: risk_type + severity + user_role → action_set (deterministic, explainable, auditable)
_metadata.ai_role: AI selects the relevant guideline, localizes it to the user's PIN/terrain/population, and simplifies the language. AI does NOT generate or invent actions.

rules.source: NDMA Flood Guidelines; MoHFW Public Health Advisory
actions: Monitor weather updates from IMD and local authorities via radio, TV, or SACHET portal.
Identify your nearest flood shelter and plan a safe evacuation route in advance.
Keep an emergency kit ready: torch, radio, medicines, dry food, drinking water, and copies of important documents.
Clear blocked drains and gutters around your home to ensure proper water runoff.
Store important documents and valuables in waterproof bags on higher shelves.

rules.source: NDMA Flood Guidelines; CWC Advisory
actions: Move valuables, important documents, and essential supplies to higher floors or elevated areas.
Avoid low-lying roads, underpasses, and waterlogged areas — do not drive through flowing water.
Keep emergency numbers accessible: NDMA (1078), local fire station, nearest hospital.
Fill clean containers with drinking water as supply lines may be disrupted.
Disconnect electrical appliances if water starts entering your home.
Follow evacuation advisories from District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA).

rules.source: NDMA Flood Guidelines; MoHFW Advisory; CWC Flood Bulletin
actions: EVACUATE to designated flood shelters immediately if advised by DDMA authorities.
Do NOT attempt to cross flowing water on foot or in a vehicle — even 15cm of moving water can knock you down.
Turn off main power switch and gas valve before leaving your home (NDMA guideline).
Carry your emergency kit: torch, medicines, dry food, drinking water, identity documents.
Do NOT go near flooded electric poles, fallen cables, or submerged electrical equipment.
After flooding: drink only boiled or chlorinated water. Do NOT use floodwater for cooking, drinking, or washing (MoHFW).
Assist elderly, disabled, and vulnerable neighbors in evacuation if it is safe to do so.
Wait for official 'All Clear' from local authorities before returning home.

rules.source: NDMA Flood Guidelines; SDRF Advisory; MoHFW
actions: EVACUATE IMMEDIATELY to the nearest designated shelter or high ground — do NOT wait.
Call NDMA Helpline 1078 or State Disaster Helpline if you are trapped.
Do NOT attempt to cross flowing water under any circumstances.
If trapped, move to the highest floor or roof and signal for help using bright cloth or torch.
Turn off all utilities (electricity, gas) before evacuation if time permits.
Do NOT return to affected areas until District Collector or DDMA declares it safe.
After rescue: seek medical attention. Watch for symptoms of waterborne diseases (diarrhea, cholera, leptospirosis).
Report dead animals or contaminated water sources to the local municipal authority.

rules.source: ICAR District Agricultural Contingency Plans; ICAR-NICRA Advisory
actions: Monitor IMD rainfall forecasts and Gramin Krishi Mausam Sewa (GKMS) advisories via Meghdoot App.
Ensure drainage channels around fields are clear and functional before the monsoon.
Avoid irrigation for 24–48 hours if heavy rainfall is predicted.
Cover stored grains and seeds with waterproof tarpaulins on raised platforms.

rules.source: ICAR DACP; ICAR Flood Advisory; State Agricultural Department
actions: Delay irrigation and postpone sowing until water recedes (ICAR advisory).
Move farm equipment, fertilizers, and pesticides to higher ground or protected storage.
Protect stored crops by elevating them above expected water level on bamboo or brick platforms.
If standing crop is at risk, consider early harvesting of mature produce to minimize losses.
Ensure livestock has access to elevated shelter and stored fodder for 3–5 days.
Contact the nearest Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) for crop insurance guidance under PMFBY.

rules.source: ICAR DACP; NDMA Flood Guidelines; State Agriculture Advisory
actions: Evacuate livestock to pre-identified elevated shelters. Carry 3–5 days of fodder and water.
Do NOT enter flooded fields — risk of drowning and contaminated water.
After floodwater recedes: assess crop damage and photograph all losses for insurance claims (PMFBY).
Plant alternate crops as recommended by KVK: hybrid maize, lentil, mustard, or short-duration vegetables.
Treat waterlogged soil with lime (as per ICAR recommendation) before replanting.
Consider waterlogging-tolerant rice varieties like Hangseswari or Saraswati for future seasons (ICAR-NICRA).

rules.source: NDMA Flood Guidelines; NCERT Disaster Management Curriculum
actions: Stay indoors and away from floodwater — it carries diseases and hidden dangers like open drains and sharp debris.
Keep school books, electronics, and important items in waterproof bags on high shelves.
Listen to parents and school authorities — follow their evacuation instructions.
Learn about your school's emergency evacuation plan and nearest safe assembly point.
Do NOT play in or near floodwater under any circumstances.
Help your family prepare an emergency kit with food, water, torch, and first-aid supplies.

rules.source: CWC Real-Time Data; NDMA Guidelines; IMD Hydromet Network
actions: Access CWC real-time flood monitoring data at cwc.gov.in for river stage levels and discharge rates.
Review IMD's Quantitative Precipitation Forecast (QPF) for basin-level rainfall projections.
Cross-reference MOSDAC satellite imagery (INSAT-3D/3DR) for rainfall estimation and cloud movement.
Analyze historical flood data from CWC for the affected basin to assess recurrence interval.
Review NDMA flash flood risk maps and GSI landslide susceptibility maps for cascading hazards.
Coordinate with State Disaster Management Authority for ground-truth validation of remote sensing data.

rules.source: NDMA Cyclone Guidelines; IMD Cyclone Advisory
actions: Monitor IMD cyclone bulletins and color-coded warnings regularly.
Prepare an emergency kit: fresh water (1 gallon/person/day for 3 days), non-perishable food, first-aid, torch, batteries.
Check your house for structural weaknesses — repair loose roofing, windows, and doors.
Identify nearest cyclone shelter and plan your evacuation route (contact DDMA for locations).

rules.source: NDMA Cyclone Guidelines; IMD Weather Warning; SDMA Advisory
actions: Secure all loose outdoor objects: flower pots, trash cans, sign boards, satellite dishes — they become lethal projectiles.
Board up windows or cover them with storm shutters. Tape large glass panes.
Charge all mobile devices and power banks to full capacity.
Stock up on essential medicines and 3-day supply of non-perishable food and clean water.
Help fishing communities secure boats and equipment in safe harbors (NDMA guideline).
Keep important documents in a waterproof bag ready for evacuation.

rules.source: NDMA Cyclone Guidelines; IMD Warning; INCOIS Storm Surge Advisory
actions: EVACUATE immediately if you are in a coastal or low-lying area as directed by DDMA.
Move to the nearest designated cyclone shelter — do NOT stay in kutcha (temporary) structures.
Stay indoors in a safe room, away from windows and glass doors.
Disconnect all electrical appliances to prevent damage from power surges.
Do NOT go outside during the 'eye' of the cyclone — winds resume suddenly from the opposite direction with greater force.
Do NOT touch fallen power lines or electrical wires.
Drink only boiled or chlorinated water to avoid contamination.
After cyclone: do NOT enter damaged buildings. Report broken utility lines to authorities.

rules.source: ICAR Advisory; NDMA Cyclone Guidelines; State Agricultural Department
actions: Harvest mature crops immediately before landfall if time permits (ICAR advisory).
Secure farm equipment and livestock in reinforced shelters away from coastal zones.
After cyclone: assess crop and soil damage. Photograph all losses for PMFBY insurance claims.
Drain excess water from fields as soon as possible to prevent root rot.
Contact your nearest KVK for advice on replanting salt-tolerant varieties after storm surge.
Apply recommended fertilizers and soil amendments per ICAR post-cyclone recovery guidelines.

rules.source: NDMA Earthquake Guidelines (2007/2016); BIS IS 1893
actions: Fasten heavy shelves and furniture securely to walls. Place heavy objects on lower shelves.
Store breakable items in low, closed cabinets with latches.
Repair defective electrical wiring and leaky gas connections immediately.
Develop a family reunification plan: decide where to meet if separated during an earthquake.
Prepare an emergency survival kit with torch, first-aid, water, dry food, whistle, and important documents.
Identify safe spots in each room: under sturdy tables, away from windows, mirrors, and heavy objects.

rules.source: NDMA Earthquake Guidelines; BIS Building Code IS 1893; SDMA Advisory
actions: Follow DROP–COVER–HOLD ON: get under sturdy furniture, cover your head, and hold on until shaking stops (NDMA).
Stay away from windows, glass doors, mirrors, and anything that could fall or shatter.
Do NOT use elevators — always use stairs for evacuation.
If outdoors: move to an open area away from buildings, trees, electric poles, and flyovers.
If driving: pull over away from buildings, bridges, and overpasses. Stay inside the vehicle.
After shaking stops: check for gas leaks. Do NOT light matches or use open flames.
Anticipate aftershocks — stay alert and be prepared to take cover again.

rules.source: NDMA Earthquake Guidelines; SDMA Advisory; BIS Standards
actions: DROP–COVER–HOLD ON immediately. Do NOT run outside during shaking.
If trapped under debris: cover mouth with cloth, tap on pipes/walls to signal rescuers. Use whistle if available. Do NOT shout (conserve air).
Do NOT re-enter damaged buildings after shaking stops — aftershocks can cause collapse.
If you smell gas: turn off main valve, leave building immediately, call fire services from outside.
Keep streets clear for emergency vehicles (NDMA guideline).
Check yourself and others for injuries. Administer first aid. Do NOT move seriously injured unless in immediate danger.
Use official information only — do NOT spread or believe rumors.
Do NOT waste water — supply lines may be damaged.

rules.source: NDMA Earthquake Guidelines; NCERT Disaster Management Curriculum; CBSE Safety Manual
actions: Follow your school's earthquake drill: DROP under your desk, COVER your head, HOLD ON.
Stay away from windows, lab equipment, bookshelves, and anything that could fall.
Do NOT run outside during shaking — wait for the teacher's instructions.
After shaking stops: evacuate the building in an orderly manner through stairs. Never use elevators.
Assemble at the designated earthquake assembly point.
If trapped: tap loudly on surface to signal rescuers. Do NOT panic.

rules.source: NDMA Heatwave Guidelines; IMD Heat Action Plan; MoHFW Advisory
actions: Drink sufficient water frequently, even if you do not feel thirsty (NDMA guideline).
Use ORS, lassi, buttermilk, lemon water, or torani (rice water) to stay hydrated.
Eat seasonal fruits with high water content: watermelon, musk melon, oranges, cucumber.
Wear lightweight, light-colored, loose-fitting cotton clothes.
Stay informed: monitor IMD heat alerts and weather updates.

rules.source: NDMA Heatwave Guidelines; IMD Color-Coded Alert; State HAP
actions: Avoid outdoor activities between 12 PM and 3 PM — the hottest part of the day.
Use goggles, umbrella/hat, and proper footwear when stepping out in the sun.
Use fans, damp clothing, and take frequent cold water baths to keep body temperature down.
Keep your home cool: use curtains/shutters, open windows at night for cooler air.
Do NOT consume alcohol, tea, coffee, or carbonated drinks — they cause dehydration (NDMA).
Never leave children or pets in a parked vehicle — temperatures rise dangerously inside.
Check on elderly neighbors living alone — they are at highest risk.

rules.source: NDMA Heatwave Guidelines; IMD Severe Heatwave Warning; MoHFW
actions: STAY INDOORS. Avoid all non-essential outdoor travel and physical exertion.
If outdoor work is unavoidable: take breaks every 20 minutes in shade, drink water continuously (NDMA).
Recognize heat stroke symptoms: high body temperature (>104°F), confusion, no sweating, rapid pulse — this is a MEDICAL EMERGENCY.
First aid for heat stroke: move person to shade, wipe body with wet cloth, pour normal temperature water on head, give ORS/lemon water, rush to nearest health center (MoHFW).
Outdoor workers: shift work to early morning or late evening. Extended breaks from noon to 4 PM (NDMA 2019 guidelines).
Do NOT eat high-protein or stale food during severe heat.
Ensure animals have shade and plenty of water.

rules.source: ICAR-NICRA Advisory; ICAR Heatwave Guidelines; KVK Advisory
actions: Apply straw mulching in orchards to reduce soil temperature and conserve moisture (ICAR).
Cover fruit trees and nurseries with shade nets (50%) to prevent sun scalding.
Irrigate crops lightly and frequently during early morning or late evening — avoid midday irrigation.
For wheat (>37°C): spray 0.2% Muriate of Potash or 2% Potassium Nitrate to prevent desiccation (ICAR advisory).
For livestock: provide shade, bathe animals twice daily, feed green fodder and mineral mixtures to minimize milk yield loss.
Avoid spraying pesticides during peak heat — chemicals become more volatile and toxic.
Contact KVK for climate-resilient variety recommendations for next season.

rules.source: NDMA Tsunami Guidelines (2010); INCOIS ITEWS Advisory
actions: If you feel a strong or prolonged earthquake near the coast, move inland to higher ground IMMEDIATELY — do NOT wait for official warning.
If you observe sudden recession or unusual rise in ocean water, move to higher ground immediately — these are natural tsunami warnings.
Tune in to radio/TV for INCOIS tsunami bulletins. Check SAMUDRA mobile app for alerts.
Move at least 500 meters inland or to elevation >10 meters above sea level.
Take emergency kit: torch, radio, medicines, documents, water.

rules.source: NDMA Tsunami Guidelines (2010); INCOIS Warning Level; SDMA Advisory
actions: EVACUATE coastal areas IMMEDIATELY. Move inland or to high ground (>10m elevation).
Do NOT go to the beach to watch waves — this is extremely dangerous.
Follow designated evacuation routes to tsunami shelters (DDMA designated locations).
Use telephones or cell phones ONLY for emergencies — conserve communication capacity (NDMA).
Do NOT return to coastal areas until DDMA/SDMA declares official 'All Clear'.
After tsunami: check for gas leaks and electrical shorts before re-entering buildings.
All food and water must be tested for contamination before use (NDMA guideline).
Stay away from debris in water — it can be lethal to boats and people.

rules.source: NDMA Landslide Guidelines; GSI Vulnerability Maps; IMD Forecast
actions: Learn warning signs: new cracks in walls/ground, tilting trees, sudden changes in stream water flow, cracking sounds.
Keep drains clear and do not dump waste on steep slopes — it destabilizes soil.
Grow deep-rooted trees and vegetation on slopes near your home (NDMA recommendation).
Store emergency supplies: food, water, flashlight, batteries, medicines, identity documents.
Stay informed: tune into IMD landslide forecasts during monsoon season.

rules.source: NDMA Landslide Guidelines; GSI Advisory; SDMA Hill Area SOPs
actions: If you notice cracks in ground, tilting structures, or unusual sounds during heavy rain, EVACUATE immediately.
Report any suspicious ground movements to the nearest government helpline or DDMA.
Do NOT stay in low-lying valleys or at the base of steep slopes during prolonged heavy rain.
Keep a disaster kit ready with essential documents in a waterproof bag.
Do NOT alter natural drainage paths or construct on slopes above 30° (NDMA 2019 recommendation).

rules.source: NDMA Landslide Guidelines; GSI SOP; SDRF Advisory
actions: EVACUATE immediately if you are in or near a landslide-prone area. Move away from the path of the slide.
Do NOT touch or walk over loose debris, exposed electric wires, or damaged poles.
Do NOT drink water from springs or wells in landslide-affected areas — it may be contaminated.
Check for injured or trapped persons ONLY if you can do so safely. Call NDMA Helpline 1078.
Stay away from the slide area until experts confirm it is safe — secondary slides are common.
Do NOT move seriously injured persons without first aid unless they are in immediate danger.

rules.source: NDMA Cold Wave Guidelines; IMD Cold Wave Advisory
actions: Wear multiple layers of loose-fitting, lightweight warm clothing. Cover head, neck, hands, and toes.
Drink hot fluids regularly (soup, warm water, ginger tea) to maintain body temperature.
Monitor IMD cold wave forecasts and color-coded weather warnings.
Check on elderly neighbors living alone — they are most vulnerable to hypothermia.

rules.source: NDMA Cold Wave Guidelines; IMD Severe Cold Wave Warning; MoHFW
actions: STAY INDOORS. Minimize travel and exposure to cold winds.
Do NOT ignore shivering — it is the first sign of dangerous heat loss. Return indoors immediately.
Do NOT drink alcohol — it reduces body temperature and increases hypothermia risk (NDMA).
Avoid wearing tight clothing — it reduces blood circulation in extremities.
Do NOT massage frostbitten skin — it causes further tissue damage. Seek medical attention.
Have an emergency kit: flashlights, batteries, extra blankets, water, non-perishable food.
For outdoor workers: take warm breaks every 30 minutes. Carry thermos with hot liquids.

rules.source: ICAR Advisory; IMD Cold Wave; State Agricultural Department
actions: Protect crops with mulching or straw cover to insulate against frost damage.
Use sprinkler irrigation in orchards during frost nights — the water layer prevents freezing damage (ICAR).
Provide adequate shelter and extra feed for livestock. Ensure warm bedding in animal shelters.
Cover nurseries and seedbeds with plastic sheets or jute to protect tender plants.
Monitor GKMS advisories on Meghdoot App for localized frost and cold wave predictions.

rules.source: CPCB National Air Quality Index (NAQI) Guidelines; MoEFCC Advisory
actions: AQI 101–200 (Moderate): Minor discomfort possible for unusually sensitive people.
Reduce prolonged outdoor exertion if you experience breathing discomfort.
Keep windows closed during peak traffic hours and in areas near industrial zones.
Monitor real-time AQI on CPCB SAMEER App or cpcb.nic.in.

rules.source: CPCB NAQI Guidelines; SAFAR Advisory; MoHFW
actions: AQI 201–300 (Poor): Breathing discomfort likely for people with lung/heart disease.
Wear an N95/P100 mask when stepping outdoors (regular cloth masks do NOT filter PM2.5).
Avoid outdoor jogging, cycling, and strenuous exercise. Shift to indoor activities.
Use air purifiers with HEPA filters indoors if available.
Keep asthma medications and inhalers accessible. Consult doctor if symptoms worsen.

rules.source: CPCB NAQI Guidelines; GRAP (Delhi NCR); SAFAR Advisory; MoHFW
actions: AQI 301–400 (Very Poor): Breathing discomfort for most people on prolonged exposure.
Wear N95 mask if heading outdoors — mandatory for outdoor workers.
Avoid ALL strenuous outdoor activities. Close windows and doors.
Use air purifiers and wet-mop floors to reduce indoor particulate matter.
Keep children, elderly, and persons with respiratory conditions strictly indoors.
If AQI exceeds 400 (Severe): call NDMA Helpline 1078. Follow GRAP emergency actions if in Delhi-NCR.

rules.source: CPCB NAQI; Delhi GRAP Guidelines; School Safety Advisory
actions: Stay indoors during school recess if AQI exceeds 300. Avoid outdoor sports and PT class.
Wear N95 mask when traveling to and from school.
If you experience difficulty breathing, wheezing, or burning eyes — report to teacher immediately.
Keep windows shut in classrooms. Request use of air purifiers if available.
Drink warm water. Jaggery and turmeric milk can help reduce throat irritation.

rules.source: ICAR DACP; ICAR-NICRA Drought Advisory; State Agriculture Department
actions: Use harvested rainwater from farm ponds for protective irrigation (ICAR recommendation).
Apply mulching with crop residues to conserve soil moisture.
Switch to short-duration and drought-tolerant crop varieties recommended by KVK.
Apply foliar spray of 2% KCl at flowering stage for rapeseed; KNO3 for cotton and soybean (ICAR).
Practice in-situ moisture conservation techniques: contour bunding, trenching, and ridge-furrow.
Contact KVK for drought contingency plan and crop insurance (PMFBY) guidance.

rules.source: ICAR DACP; ICAR-CRIDA Advisory; State Agricultural Contingency Plan
actions: Prioritize life-saving irrigation for standing crops. Use drip irrigation to maximize efficiency.
Harvest whatever produce is salvageable — do not wait for full maturity in severe drought.
Consider fodder crops (sorghum, bajra) for livestock if main crop is lost.
Apply for crop loss compensation under PMFBY — document all damage with photographs.
Reduce livestock herd to manageable numbers. Prioritize milch animals and breeding stock.
Contact Block Development Officer (BDO) for access to fodder banks and cattle camps.

rules.source: NDMA Drought Guidelines; Central Ground Water Board Advisory
actions: Conserve water: fix leaking taps, use bucket instead of hose, reuse kitchen water for plants.
Harvest rainwater using rooftop collection systems if possible.
Follow local water rationing schedules issued by the municipal corporation.
Store drinking water in clean, covered containers — boil water if quality is suspect.
Report suspected water contamination to local authorities immediately.

rules.source: NDMA Chemical Disaster Guidelines; Factory Act 1948; PESO Advisory
actions: Move UPWIND from the chemical release site — never downwind.
Cover nose and mouth with a wet cloth as an emergency measure against toxic fumes.
Do NOT touch spilled chemicals or contaminated materials.
If chemicals have contacted your skin: remove contaminated clothing, wash skin with clean water for 15 minutes.
Call NDMA Helpline 1078 and local fire services. Report the chemical identity if known.
Stay at least 500 meters from the incident site. Follow local authority's evacuation orders.
After incident: do NOT consume food, water, or crops from contaminated areas until cleared by authorities.

rules.source: NDMA Lightning/Thunderstorm Advisory; IMD Nowcast Warning
actions: Move indoors or into a hard-topped vehicle immediately. Avoid open fields, hilltops, and isolated trees.
Disconnect electrical appliances and avoid using corded phones during lightning.
Stay away from metallic fences, poles, and water bodies.
If no shelter: crouch low on balls of your feet, keep feet together, cover ears. Do NOT lie flat.
Wait 30 minutes after the last thunder before going outside (30-30 rule).
Farmers: do NOT stand near tractors, metal implements, or under isolated trees during lightning.

rules.source: NDMA Lightning Advisory; ICAR; IMD Nowcast
actions: Stop all field work immediately when lightning is nearby. Move to enclosed shelter.
Do NOT stand under isolated trees or near metal equipment (tractors, ploughs, fencing).
Secure livestock in covered shelters — do NOT leave them in open fields.
After storm: check for downed power lines and report to electricity board.
Inspect crops for hail damage. Photograph damage for insurance documentation under PMFBY.
